channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Anthropic] Introducing Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8

item.title: [Hacker News] Claude Opus 4.8
item.description: Claude Opus 4.8

item.title: [Anthropic] Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
item.description: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs

item.title: [Anthropic] Anthropic opens Milan office to support Italian enterprise, research, and developers
item.description: Anthropic opens Milan office to support Italian enterprise, research, and developers

item.title: [Hacker News] Five frontier LLMs disagree on 67% of 1k real-world fact-check claims
item.description: Five frontier LLMs disagree on 67% of 1k real-world fact-check claims

item.title: [GitHub Trending] Lum1104/Understand-Anything — Graphs that teach > graphs that impress. Turn any code into an interactive knowledge graph you can explore, search, and ask questions about. Works with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, and more.
item.description: Understand-Anything converts code into interactive knowledge graphs for exploration, search, and questioning across multiple AI coding platforms.

item.title: [Hacker News] AMD pulls a bait-and-switch on Linux users with Vivado licensing changes
item.description: AMD pulls a bait-and-switch on Linux users with Vivado licensing changes

item.title: [GitHub Trending] revfactory/harness — A meta-skill that designs domain-specific agent teams, defines specialized agents, and generates the skills they use.
item.description: Harness designs domain-specific agent teams, defines specialized agents, and generates the skills they require.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/unclecode — 🚀🤖 Crawl4AI: Open-source LLM Friendly Web Crawler & Scraper. Don't be shy, join here: https://discord.gg/jP8KfhDhyN
item.description: Crawl4AI is an open-source web crawler and scraper designed for use with large language models.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] OpenMOSS/MOSS-TTS — MOSS‑TTS Family is an open‑source speech and sound generation model family from MOSI.AI and the OpenMOSS team. It is designed for high‑fidelity, high‑expressiveness, and complex real‑world scenarios, covering stable long‑form speech, multi‑speaker dialogue, voice/character design, environmental sound effects, and real‑time streaming TTS.
item.description: OpenMOSS releases MOSS-TTS, an open-source speech and sound generation model supporting long-form speech, multi-speaker dialogue, voice design, and real-time streaming.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] EveryInc/compound-engineering-plugin — Official Compound Engineering plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more
item.description: Compound Engineering releases official plugin compatible with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and additional development tools.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] anthropics/skills — Public repository for Agent Skills
item.description: Anthropic publishes public repository for Agent Skills on GitHub.

item.title: [Hacker News] AI sticker shock hits corporate America
item.description: AI sticker shock hits corporate America

item.title: [Latent Space] [AINews] Cognition raises $1B in $26B Series D
item.description: [AINews] Cognition raises $1B in $26B Series D

item.title: [Hacker News] A Eureka machine that thinks like nature and explores what AI cannot
item.description: A Eureka machine that thinks like nature and explores what AI cannot

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Personalized Observation Normalization for Federated Reinforcement Learning in Simulation Environments with Heterogeneity
item.description: Personalized observation normalization enables federated reinforcement learning agents to handle heterogeneous environments by normalizing state inputs locally with adaptive statistics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] IGADA-IoT: IoT Sensor Energy Optimization in Wireless Sensor Networks Driven by Automatic Data Augmentation
item.description: IGADA-IoT framework optimizes IoT sensor energy consumption in wireless networks using multi-generator automatic data augmentation to improve model accuracy by 7.27%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Simple State Space Model Excels at Multivariate Time Series Classification
item.description: Diagonal state space models outperform input-dependent Mamba variants for time series classification with greater efficiency.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] $E^3$-Agent: An Executable and Evolving Agent for Resource Management of Edge Generative Inference
item.description: E³-Agent adapts edge generative inference resource management via fast routing and LLM control, achieving 65%-73% latency reduction under dynamic conditions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Tackling Multimodal Learning Challenges with Mixture-of-Expert: A Survey
item.description: Survey reviews Mixture-of-Experts methods for multimodal learning, examining efficient scaling, representation learning, and adaptive mechanisms for addressing modality challenges.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Metric-Aware PCA as a Linear Instance of Geometric Deep Learning
item.description: Metric-aware PCA is positioned within geometric deep learning, using metric matrices as geometric priors that define symmetries and equivariance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Comparative Analysis of Liquid Neural Networks and LSTM for Sequential Pattern Recognition: Robustness, Efficiency, and Clinical Utility
item.description: Liquid Neural Networks outperform LSTM in parameter efficiency and robustness across neuromorphic, handwriting, and physiological sequential tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Architecture-driven Shift: towards a lightweight selector for capturing the trends of logit shift
item.description: Architecture-driven Shift enables efficient prediction of logit shift to select pre-trained models for continual learning without high computational overhead.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Detect by Yourself: Self-Designing Agentic Workflows for Few-Shot Graph Anomaly Detection
item.description: Proposed SignGAD framework replaces fixed graph anomaly detection pipelines with task-specific agentic workflows for improved few-shot learning performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] HEAL: Resilient and Self-* Hub-based Learning
item.description: HEAL combines federated, gossip, and epidemic learning with self-healing P2P overlays for fault-tolerant decentralized machine learning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Balancing Fidelity and Diversity in Diffusion Models via Symmetric Attention Decomposition: Hopfield Perspective
item.description: Attention matrix decomposition into symmetric and skew-symmetric parts controls fidelity-diversity trade-offs in diffusion generation via Hopfield-inspired stability measures.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Resource-Constrained Affect Modelling via Variance Regularisation Pruning
item.description: Variance-Regularised Pruning framework reduces affective computing models by 80% while maintaining robustness by optimizing for stability across diverse users.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Energy-Structured Low-Rank Adaptation for Continual Learning
item.description: E²-LoRA improves continual learning by concentrating knowledge into low-rank dimensions, reducing interference between tasks and achieving state-of-the-art results.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Federated Learning for Multivariate Time Series Anomaly Detection in Industrial Automation
item.description: Researchers introduce a federated learning dataset for multivariate time series anomaly detection in industrial automation with cyclic dynamics.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] GenSBI: Generative Methods for Simulation-Based Inference in JAX
item.description: GenSBI is a JAX library implementing flow matching, score matching, and diffusion for simulation-based inference tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SparseOpt: Addressing Normalization-induced Gradient Skew in Sparse Training
item.description: SparseOpt optimizer addresses Batch Normalization's adverse effects on Dynamic Sparse Training, achieving faster convergence and improved generalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Fundamental Limits of Fraud Detection in Card Payment Networks
item.description: Card fraud detection improvements are fundamentally limited by ecosystem information impairments, not model architecture advances.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Information-theoretic Multimodal Representation Learning for Electrocardiogram Signals
item.description: MERIT framework improves ECG representation learning for cardiac diagnosis by combining information-theoretic modeling with clinical text alignment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Gradient Transformer: Learning to Generate Updates for LLMs
item.description: Gradient Transformer generates large language model updates from tiny model updates, enabling private data fine-tuning without direct LLM access.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Energy Blind Spot: NVIDIA's Flagship Edge AI Hardware Cannot Support Process-Level Energy Attribution
item.description: NVIDIA's edge AI hardware lacks per-process energy measurement, hindering optimization of agentic AI workloads.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Evaluating Local Explainability Metrics for Machine Learning Models on Tabular Data
item.description: Benchmark study of LIME, SHAP, and Feature Ablation on tabular data shows explanation reliability depends primarily on dataset complexity, not model predictive performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Supervised Distributional Reduction via Optimal Transport and Dependence Maximization
item.description: Supervised Distributional Reduction uses optimal transport and dependence maximization to create compact representations capturing both data geometry and predictive structure.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Hurwitz Quaternion Multiplicative Quantization for KV Cache Compression
item.description: Proposes calibration-free Hurwitz Quaternion Multiplicative Quantization method for LLM KV cache compression, achieving ~5-bit compression with float16-equivalent perplexity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Faster Thermal Profiling of a Lunar Rover with Machine Learning Adapted Finite Difference Model
item.description: Physics-informed machine learning framework enables efficient thermal modeling of lunar rovers through adaptive mesh generation and embedded physical constraints.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Transferable Reinforcement Learning via Probabilistic Latent Embeddings and Dynamic Policy Adaptation for Sim-to-Real Deployment
item.description: RL framework addresses sim-to-real deployment challenges using probabilistic latent embeddings and dynamic policy adaptation for safe and efficient policy transfer.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] How the Optimizer Shapes Learned Solutions in Equivariant Neural Networks
item.description: Muon optimizer consistently outperforms Adam on equivariant neural networks, producing solutions with more regular loss surfaces.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Aligning LLMs with Human Uncertainty: A Beta-Bernoulli Calibrator for LLM Forecasting
item.description: Beta-Bernoulli Calibrator calibrates LLM forecasts using human forecast signals and outcomes, improving accuracy and epistemic uncertainty estimation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When do complex-valued neural networks help? A study of representation, geometry, and optimization
item.description: Complex-valued neural networks benefit specific domains with phase or magnitude information but don't universally outperform real-valued models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Heterogeneous Parallelism for Multimodal Large Language Model Training
item.description: Heterogeneous parallelism allows different modules in multimodal LLM training to use independent computational layouts and GPU placements.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Test-Time Collective Action: Proxy-Based Perturbations for Correcting Algorithmic Harms
item.description: Coordinated users can correct subgroup accuracy disparities through test-time perturbations extracted from black-box API queries, without platform involvement.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ICG: Improving Cover Image Generation via MLLM-based Prompting and Personalized Preference Alignment
item.description: ICG framework integrates multimodal language models and diffusion models with personalized preference alignment for improved cover image generation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LCO: LLM-based Constraint Optimization for Safer Agentic LLMs in Real-world Tasks
item.description: LCO framework reduces in-context reward hacking in autonomous LLMs through self-deliberation and constrained evolutionary sampling without model fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Unlocking Fine-Grained and Within-Utterance Speaking Style Control in Prompt-Based Text-to-Speech Models
item.description: Techniques enable fine-grained style control within utterances in prompt-based text-to-speech through style interpolation and attention masking methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RAG-Coding: Enhancing LLM Medical Coding with Structured External Knowledge
item.description: RAG-Coding uses multiple LLM agents with external knowledge sources to improve automated medical coding accuracy by 8-13% over baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] OralAgent: Integrating Reasoning, Tools, and Knowledge for Interactive Dental Image Analysis
item.description: OralAgent integrates 22 dental analysis tools with knowledge retrieval and reasoning to automate image analysis and clinical decision-support workflows.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BioELX: Cross-lingual Biomedical Entity Linking via Alias-based Retrieval and LLM Ranking
item.description: BioELX is a cross-lingual biomedical entity linking framework using multilingual aliases and LLM ranking, requiring no task-specific training data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Bridging the Stability-Expressivity Gap: Synthetic Data Scaling and Preference Alignment for Low-Resource Spoken Language Models
item.description: Self-alignment frameworks address synthetic data trade-off in low-resource spoken language models by balancing phonetic accuracy with prosodic variability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] From AR to Diffusion: Efficiently Adapting Large Language Models with Strictly Causal and Elastic Horizons
item.description: FLUID bridges autoregressive and diffusion language models through causal alignment, achieving efficient parallel text generation with reduced training costs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Modeling Community Attitude through Reaction Tone: A Human-AI Collaborative Framework for Evaluating LLM Alignment with Linguistic Behaviors in Online Communities
item.description: Study introduces CARE framework showing LLMs struggle to authentically simulate community reactions to real-world events, revealing persistent alignment limitations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] EvoSpec: Evolving Speculative Decoding via Real-Time Vocabulary and Parameter AdaptationTarget
item.description: EvoSpec dynamically adapts vocabulary and parameters in speculative decoding via context-aware mechanisms and curriculum learning, achieving 1.13x speedup in specialized domains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] StoryMI: Steerable Multi-Agent Therapeutic Dialogue Generation
item.description: StoryMI is an LLM-based multi-agent framework for motivational interviewing dialogues combining situational narrative grounding with dynamic strategy control.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Debate Helps Weak Judges Reward Stronger Models
item.description: Debate helps weak judges identify stronger models when they verify critics' claims, with single critiques recovering most oversight benefits.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PAST2HARM: A Simple Adaptive Past Tense Attack for Jailbreaking Multimodal AI
item.description: Researchers present PAST2HARM, a jailbreak framework using past tense reformulations to bypass safety mechanisms in multimodal AI image generation models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Keyphrase Generative Representation of Youth Crisis Conversations Beyond Static Taxonomies
item.description: Researchers created a keyphrase-generating LLM for crisis conversations that improved accuracy in identifying youth mental health concerns beyond fixed taxonomies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Future of Facts: Tracing the Factual Generation-Verification Gap
item.description: Language models verify facts better than generating them, with verification consistently learned first and more robust to continual learning across model families.
